macro: Monday open after Friday's rout: the hot May jobs report (172k vs ~88k est.) keeps the 10-year near 4.54% and markets now price a better than 60% chance the Fed hikes by year-end, with the June 16-17 meeting in focus. The Strait of Hormuz escalation (US disabled a tanker; Iran launched a retaliatory drone strike on Kuwait International Airport) holds Brent near $93 and adds an inflation risk premium that is directly relevant to GCC markets. Semis remain the pressure point after Broadcom's soft AI outlook erased ~$350B of market cap, while BTC is stabilising near $61.5k after Friday's flush to $59.2k.

NVDA.name: Nvidia
NVDA.note: Fell -6.2% Fri to $205.10 as a hot May jobs report (172k vs 88k est) fuelled Fed hike bets and Broadcom's weak AI chip outlook hit semis. Now -13% from $235.74 ATH. Watch $200, re-entry only if selling stabilises; long-term AI thesis intact.

MSFT.name: Microsoft
MSFT.note: Down ~13% YTD; commentary notes its own reasoning models reduce OpenAI dependence, a potential long-term margin positive. Rate-hike repricing remains the near-term driver into the Jun 16-17 Fed meeting.

INTC.name: Intel
INTC.note: Crashed -11.3% Fri to $99.17 in the semiconductor rout (Micron -17%, AMD -12.6% over two days). Still up >2x from March lows; extreme volatility, do not chase, wait for base above $100.

AVGO.name: Broadcom
AVGO.note: Beat on Jun 3 (EPS 2.44 vs 2.40 est) but a softer AI outlook triggered a 19.9% three-session slide to 385.73. High beta; next report Sep 3. No new entries until selling stabilises.

QQQ.name: Nasdaq-100 ETF
QQQ.note: Promoted to full 3Y live history on the FMP Starter tier. Fell 4.8% Friday in the semi-led rout but holds +15% YTD; core growth sleeve alongside SPY. Watch the 700 level.

SMH.name: Semiconductor ETF
SMH.note: Worst day of the year Friday, -9.2%, after Broadcom's soft AI outlook and hot jobs data hit semis. Concentrated exposure; satellite sizing only, wait for stabilisation.

SPY.name: S&P 500 ETF
SPY.note: Fell -2.6% Fri to $737.55, S&P's worst day since Apr 2025 as strong jobs data lifted 10Y yield to 4.54% and raised Fed hike odds. Core long-term holding; staged buying on dips remains the plan.

GOLD.name: Gold (oz)
GOLD.note: Dropped -3.1% to $4,365 as surging yields hit non-yielding assets. Down from $5,242 March peak. Hedge role intact but rate-hike repricing is a near-term headwind.

BTC.name: Bitcoin
BTC.note: Stabilising near $61.5k (FMP snapshot $61.8k intraday Sunday) after the overnight low of $59.2k; ETF outflows, higher oil and quant-fund deleveraging keep pressure on. Prediction markets still see ~80% odds of a sub-$60k print in 2026.

long.h: Nasdaq plunges 4% as AI trade halts on Fed hike bets
long.s: Hot May payrolls (172k, ~2x expected) lifted the 10-year to 4.54% and sparked the worst session of the year.

long.h: Fed seen holding June 16-17; >60% odds of a hike by year-end
long.s: S&P 500 snapped a nine-week win streak (-2.5% on the week) as rate-hike odds rose on strong jobs data.

long.h: Hormuz escalation keeps Brent near $93, adds inflation risk
long.s: US disabled a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz; Iran answered with a drone strike on Kuwait International Airport.

long.h: Broadcom called the canary in the AI coal mine after guide cut
long.s: AVGO's soft full-year revenue outlook erased ~$350B in value and reset expectations across AI semis.

long.h: Microsoft's distance from OpenAI framed as investor positive
long.s: MSFT's own reasoning models cut reliance on OpenAI; the stock is down more than 13% this year.

short.h: Bitcoin steadies near $61.5k after overnight drop to $59.2k
short.s: BTC collapsed from ~$73.8k to a $59.1k low last week; now consolidating just above $61k.

short.h: Bitcoin's break below $64,000 triggered $1.1B in liquidations
short.s: Leveraged longs were flushed within 24 hours as the AI-trade rollover spilled into crypto.

short.h: ETF outflows and higher oil keep BTC under pressure
short.s: Sustained spot-ETF outflows plus an energy-driven inflation impulse weigh on crypto risk appetite.

short.h: Traders forecast new 2026 lows for bitcoin after sell-off
short.s: Prediction markets put ~80% odds on BTC falling below $60,000 during 2026.

short.h: Leveraged semi ETFs warned of another brutal session Monday
short.s: After June 5's wipeout, 2x semiconductor products face elevated gap risk into the June 8 open.

vix.note: CBOE VIX via FMP index-quote (free tier); auto-activates vix_spike_regime when px>=28 or day>=30
